commands.review_summary:
The PR adds gateway startup warnings for configured channels whose owning channel plugin is not loadable under the activation/trust policy, plus focused tests and activation-aware config threading.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +134. Total +173 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can mark a configured global/config-origin channel owner as blocked by untrusted-plugin while the startup logger has no configured-channel warning path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer/contributor follow-up is needed for real startup proof and for deciding whether this warning-only PR should close the broader linked compatibility issue.

target.title: fix: warn when configured channel plugins cannot load
target.body:
Fixes #91873

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with a configured external Slack channel could restart the gateway after an upgrade and see a normal ready banner even though the channel plugin was not loadable under the current plugin trust policy.

## Why This Change Was Made

The change keeps the explicit plugin trust boundary intact and makes the failure mode visible during gateway startup. The startup log now evaluates configured-channel plugin availability with the same activation-aware config used by plugin startup, so auto-enabled healthy channels do not produce false warnings.

## User Impact

Operators now get a loud startup warning when a configured channel is present but no channel plugin is loadable. The warning points them to `openclaw doctor --fix` or the plugin trust settings instead of leaving the channel silently absent from the loaded plugin list.

## Evidence

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.vitest-cache-91873 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-import-boundary.test.ts`  
  Result: 6 files passed, 126 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`  
  Result: all matched files use the correct format.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`  
  Result: passed.
- `git diff --check`  
  Result: passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`  
  Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway startup now logs a warning when a configured channel has no loadable channel plugin under the startup activation policy.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on macOS with Node v24.15.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.vitest-cache-91873 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-import-boundary.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`; `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix: The focused gateway Vitest shard passed 6 files and 126 tests, including assertions that a blocked configured Slack channel emits a startup warning, ANSI/control characters in channel ids are sanitized, and activation-aware startup config suppresses false warnings for enabled channel owners.

Observed result after fix: A configured-but-unloadable channel no longer disappears behind only the normal ready banner; startup emits a warning with the configured channel id and remediation path.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Slack Socket Mode upgrade or external Slack API connection; this PR proves the gateway startup warning and activation-policy behavior in focused source tests.
